source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Pipe Skate

event.top_event: Dropped drill pipe from 33 feet onto pipe skate
actions_taken: Manufacturer technician brought onboard for system tuning
Further training provided to drill crews on PIMS software operation
event.summary: On January 26, 2022, at 0106 hours, the Driller on the Diamond Offshore Black Lion drillship inadvertently opened the drill pipe elevators instead of the pipe slips while hoisting a single joint of 6-5/8 inch drill pipe approximately 33 feet in the air. The drill pipe dropped back onto the pipe skate, coming to rest diagonally approximately 4 feet from the Skate Operator. No injuries were reported. The incident was classified as a high potential near miss.
recommendations: Ensure PIMS software is properly commissioned and drill crews are thoroughly trained on initiation, use, and troubleshooting
Verify mechanical failsafe pin activation in elevators prior to lifting operations
Consider redesign of joysticks on Driller's chair to differentiate identical controls performing different functions

hazards.name: Dropped object hazard from suspended drill pipe
Uncontrolled release of drill pipe from lifting equipment

threats.name: Driller inadvertently activates wrong joystick control
Software interlock system not activated prior to lift
Mechanical failsafe pin not engaged due to insufficient pipe height

consequences.name: Dropped drill pipe strikes personnel or equipment
Injury or fatality to Skate Operator
Structural damage to pipe skate and equipment

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) and Site-Specific Procedure (SSP) for Picking up Tubulars
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to establish safe work procedures

supporting_text: A Job Safety Analyses (JSA) on 'Picking up Tubulars from the Catwalk' and a site-specific procedure (SSP), 'Pick up Tubulars from Pipe Catwalk' was reviewed and signed by all participating drill crew members
The Driller inadvertently clicked the wrong joystick and opened the drill pipe elevators

controls.name: Pipe Interlock Management System (PIMS) software interlock
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent unintended elevator opening
controls.lod_basis: Cyberbased system designed to prevent operator error

supporting_text: The software interlock, part of the Pipe Interlock Management System (PIMS), was not activated within the system prior to picking up the drill pipe off the skate
The Driller failed to activate the 'Well Center Standbuilding' mode within the manufactures cyberbased system that allows the software interlock to function
During the initial installation, the software was not sufficiently commissioned, and the drill crew was not thoroughly trained on how to initiate, use and troubleshoot the system

controls.name: Mechanical failsafe pin in elevator inserts
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to mechanically lock elevators when pipe is vertical
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical interlock activated when box end of tool joint sits vertically in elevator inserts

supporting_text: The mechanical failsafe pin inside the elevators was not activated
The elevators were still 'Link Tilted' out, towards the pipe skate and the pipe was not vertical enough for the pin to activate
The pipe would have had to be 35 to 38 feet high for the pin to interlock on the elevators

controls.name: Pre-tour briefing and crew handover
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure crew awareness and readiness
controls.lod_basis: Shift briefing and crew transition protocol

supporting_text: At midnight on January 26, 2022, the auxiliary 'C' drill crew came on tour after completing a briefing with the drill crew coming off tour

controls.name: Tail arm stabilization procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to stabilize pipe during lifting
controls.lod_basis: Procedural step to stabilize drill pipe as it is positioned over rotary

supporting_text: he stopped to allow the Skate Operator to engage the tail arm to stabilize the drill pipe until it is placed in the rotary

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
